title: The Room of Rotating Theses

features.name: thesis-house
features.desc-on-touch: A shopfront that argues one thing. When you read its windows, its statement is clear and final. But the building next to it argues the opposite with equal certainty.

features.name: antithesis-house
features.desc-on-touch: This building argues the negation. Nothing in its structure contradicts the thesis-house. Both are stable. The archive learned that two opposite truths can hold the same weight.

features.name: paradox-pivot
features.desc-on-touch: A rotating mechanism. It spins the room so thesis becomes antithesis becomes synthesis becomes absurdity becomes thesis again. The rotation never stops. It is the only stable thing here.

features.name: contradiction-glyph
features.desc-on-touch: Writing that reads as true and false in the same moment. The archive can hold it. The script seems to turn inside out as you watch.

features.name: logic-monument
features.desc-on-touch: A wireframe structure that proves nothing and everything at once. The archive built this to hold thought that contradicts itself and remains coherent.

inscription: in this room thesis and antithesis waltz together; they have learned that being right and wrong simultaneously is the most stable position; paradox became architecture

file.name: Paradox_Stability_Proof_AllContradictionsCanCoexist.txt
file.note: A mathematical argument that something is both true and false. The archive filed it in the logic section and the absurdity section simultaneously. Both are correct.

exits.label: into the chamber where statement becomes counterstatement becomes statement
outward where theses separate and become unsure of themselves again
downward into deeper paradox where contradiction becomes indistinguishable from truth
upward toward resolution that resolves into paradox again

reverie.title: REVERIE — trying to argue when contradiction is stable
narr: You try to tell her she's wrong. She argues back.
But the room rotates. Suddenly she's right and you're
wrong. Then it spins again. You're both right. You're
both wrong. She stops arguing and so do you. The archive
learned that in a place where opposites hold equal weight,
the only stable thing is to stop trying to decide.
